game_name: Tactics Ogre: Let Us Cling Together

design_philosophy.core_loop: Position units on grid-based maps, manage turn order via Wait Turn system, exploit terrain/weather/alignment interactions to achieve battle objectives while building character classes and army composition
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strategic positioning and resource management (unit placement, class evolution, equipment) balanced against narrative consequence (branching story paths with permanent character outcomes)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a customizable squad through a war-torn kingdom, making morally significant choices that reshape the narrative and determine which allies survive
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces narrative agency through irreversible alignment choices (Law/Neutral/Chaos) affecting character availability and story branching; chess-inspired tactical system prioritizes positioning over real-time action; permadeath of protagonist forces consequence management; elemental affinity and weather create emergent strategic depth

difficulty.lives_system: Single life for protagonist Denim; if killed, game must restart from last save point
difficulty.checkpointing: Save point-based; players can quicksave between story events and battles
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through unit recruitment (Persuade ability to convert enemies), class evolution system, equipment optimization, and training mode allowing pre-battle squad preparation and unlimited practice battles

progression.upgrade_system: Character class evolution through battle use; classes transfer between compatible units. Individual unit experience level progression. Equipment (weapons, armor, accessories) upgrades through pre-battle loadout customization
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression gates map nodes on world map; branching paths (Law/Neutral/Chaos) unlock different story sequences and make certain characters recruitable or permanently dead. Tarot card drops from defeated enemies provide temporary battle boosts
replayability_hooks: Three distinct narrative branches (Law/Neutral/Chaos) with different character fates and story outcomes
Training mode with two-player multiplayer support allowing unlimited squad composition experimentation
Character recruitment via Persuade mechanic creates variable army compositions across playthroughs
Class evolution system encourages replaying battles with different unit combinations

world_building_techniques: Dialogue-driven cutscenes presenting character choice moments that branch narrative
Player input during character creation (name, birthday, alignment questions) personalizes story path
Permanent character death/recruitment tied to alignment choices creates emergent narrative stakes
Story context before and after each battle sequence frames military conflicts within personal/political drama
Ethnic conflict and occupying powers in Valeria establish geopolitical narrative depth inspired by real-world European and Asian conflicts

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters function as story checkpoints and class ability showcases; aligned with narrative progression and character development arcs

faction_indicators: Law alignment: accepting authority commands, preserves more allied characters
Neutral alignment: middle path with moderate character availability
Chaos alignment: rejecting authority, enables recruitment of antagonistic characters
danger_signals: Protagonist death forces complete restart from save, creating high consequence for tactical failures
Permanent character loss from alignment choices means allies available in one playthrough vanish in another
Elemental affinity mismatches reduce unit effectiveness, punishing party composition errors
environmental_storytelling: Terrain elevation and weather conditions change battle map strategy and provide narrative flavor (occupation forces versus resistance fighting in different environmental contexts)
World map day-cycle connected to movement creates temporal narrative progression
Grid-based overhead perspective grounds conflict in tactical geography of Valeria
